name: Crockpot Stuffed Turkey Breast
recipeIngredient: Cheesecloth (about 24x36 inches for each turkey breast)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine melted butter, chopped onion, celery, bacon croutons, chicken bouillon, parsley, and poultry seasoning.
2. Cut the turkey breast into thick slices from the breastbone to the rib cage, leaving the slices attached to the bone.
3. Sprinkle salt and pepper on the turkey slices.
4. Soak cheesecloth in white wine.
5. Place the turkey on a layer of cheesecloth.
6. Stuff each slice with bread mixture.
7. Fold one end of the cheesecloth over the other to cover the meat.
8. Put the wrapped turkey on a metal rack or trivet in the slow-cooking pot.
9. Cover the pot and cook on low for 7 to 9 hours, or until tender.
10. Add more wine during cooking if desired.
11. Remove from the pot and take off the cheesecloth immediately.
12. If a browner breast is preferred, remove the turkey from the pot and brown in an oven set to 400 degrees for 15 to 20 minutes.
13. Let stand for 10 minutes before serving.
14. Serve each person one or more thick slices of turkey with dressing in between.
This recipe is designed for a 4 1/2 quart or larger slow-cooking pot.
